name: Barranca Valley Trail
name_original: Parque Nacional de Guadarrama – circular por la Senda Ortiz y de La Barranca
description: Hike a moderate 5.8-mile loop in Guadarrama National Park, featuring pine forests, panoramic views from Mirador de las Canchas, and varied t
region_intro: Hiking around Navacerrada offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, a region characterized by pine forests, mountain passes, and granite formations. The area features prominent peaks like Siete Picos and Bola del Mundo, alongside valleys and reservoirs such as La Barranca and Embalse de Navacerrada. This natural setting provides varied terrain for outdoor activities, making it a popular destination for those seeking routes in Navacerrada. Best hiking trails around Navacerrada The most popular hiking route is Barranca Valley Trail, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This route offers a picturesque mix of wooded and rugged landscapes within La Barranca Valley. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Maliciosa y Bola del Mundo — circular desde La Barranca, a difficult 9.1 miles (14.6 km) path. This challenging route provides panoramic views of the Sierra de Guadarrama and ascends two majestic peaks. Local hikers also love the La Barranca - La Maliciosa Trail, a 6.9 miles (11.2 km) trail leading through the La Barranca Valley and towards the iconic La Maliciosa, often completed in about 5 hours 2 minutes. Hiking around Navacerrada is defined by majestic peaks, pine forests, and serene reservoirs. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to demanding ascents. The routes in Navacerrada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 1,100 reviews. More than 4,600 hikers have used komoot to explore Navacerrada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Navacerrada?
A: Navacerrada offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 90 distinct routes available for exploration. These trails cater to different skill levels, from easy walks to challenging ascents within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking routes in Navacerrada?
A: Yes, Navacerrada provides several easy and family-friendly options. A great choice is the circular route around the Navacerrada Reservoir, which offers spectacular views of the Sierra de Guadarrama with minimal elevation gain. You can explore a route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1317144413/return-to-the-navacerrada-reservoir-from-navacerrada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Navacerrada reservoir from Navacerrada&lt;/a&gt; for a gentle experience.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Navacerrada?
A: Hiking in Navacerrada takes you through diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. You'll encounter beautiful pine forests, majestic peaks like Siete Picos and Bola del Mundo, and serene valleys such as La Barranca. The area is also characterized by impressive granite formations and offers fresh mountain air.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Navacerrada?
A: Yes, Navacerrada features several circular routes. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1318120992/camorritos-loop-from-navacerrada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camorritos Loop from Navacerrada&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through varied terrain. The region's topography, including reservoirs and valleys, naturally lends itself to many loop trails.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for?
A: Navacerrada is rich in natural landmarks. You can explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3692534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalara Glacial Cirque&lt;/a&gt; with its striking glacial formations, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navacerrada Pass&lt;/a&gt;, a significant mountain pass offering panoramic views. The region also boasts majestic peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/121253" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seven Peaks&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1002532" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Maliciosa Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest along the trails?
A: Yes, for those interested in history, the region includes the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/641391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Road of Puerto de la Fuenfría&lt;/a&gt;, a well-preserved section of an ancient Roman road that you can incorporate into your hike.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Navacerrada?
A: The hiking trails around Navacerrada are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 1,100 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the fresh mountain air, and the variety of routes suitable for all levels, from serene reservoir walks to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Navacerrada?
A: Navacerrada offers beautiful hiking experiences throughout the year. Spring and summer are ideal for enjoying the lush greenery and warmer temperatures. Autumn brings vibrant colors to the forests, making it a picturesque time for walks. Even in winter, with proper gear, some trails offer stunning snow-covered vistas, especially around the higher peaks.

Q: Are there any lakes or reservoirs I can visit while hiking?
A: Absolutely. The region features several beautiful bodies of water. The Navacerrada Reservoir is popular for easy circular walks, offering scenic views. Other notable spots include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2877584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of the Birds&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4559864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Air Force Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which provide tranquil settings for hikers.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter in the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park?
A: The Sierra de Guadarrama National Park is home to diverse wildlife. While hiking, you might spot wild boar, foxes, roe deer, and squirrels. The park is also a haven for various bird species, making it an interesting area for nature observation.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced mountaineers?
A: Yes, Navacerrada is known for its demanding ascents. Peaks like Siete Picos and La Maliciosa offer challenging routes with significant elevation gain and impressive granite formations, providing rewarding experiences for experienced hikers seeking a strenuous workout and breathtaking views.
tips: The Senda Ortiz maintains a path halfway up the slope from Las Vueltas de la Barranca to the site of the old Guadarrama Hospital, which has now completely disappeared. On the esplanade where the building was, there are two information panels and good views of some mountain villages. Long before reaching the viewpoint of the old hospital, in the clearing of the Canto Gordo pass, a narrow, stony path starts to the right and climbs up the slope of the forest to the Las Canchas or La Barranca viewpoint.

The Las Canchas viewpoint is an extraordinary panoramic point of the Barranca Valley, enclosed by the Guarramillas and Maliciosa massifs. The enigmatic and mysterious name of Maliciosa appears on several occasions in local chronicles from the fourteenth century and is usually related to tragic events that occurred to countrymen from nearby towns. The best known were the murders of the bandit Pablo Santos, in the Peña del Mediodía; of the shepherd nicknamed El Mirlo, on the Valdehalcones pass; and of another shepherd with an unknown name who died in the Peña de la Barranca.

Looking face to face at the mountain from the southern slope, it is easy to agree with the shepherds of yesteryear and “like Madaleta in the Pyrenees, Maliciosa is in Guadarrama, the cursed one; land of the most mistreated in the Sierra, where the rock, always desolate and naked, which time continuously sharpens and wears away, dies in its beauty, deeper and deeper”. The Pedriza del Real de Manzanares. Constancio Bernaldo de Quiros. Uneven Editions 1999.

The water from this source is one of the best of all the sources in the Sierra de Guadarrama. Every time I stop here I empty whatever water I bring with me to refill with the fresh mountain water that comes out of the spout.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: This circular route, covering approximately 9.3 kilometers, generally takes about 2.5 to 3.5 hours to complete. The exact duration can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain and difficulty like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain, combining wide forest tracks with narrower, sometimes stony paths. While considered moderate overall, some sections, especially along the Senda Ortiz, can be technical or steep with roots and rocks, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route conveniently starts near a parking lot in the Valle de la Barranca area. This makes it easy to access the trailhead and begin your hike.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable spots. Highlights include the scenic Senda Ortiz, the stunning Las Canchas Viewpoint with panoramic vistas of the Barranca Valley and Maliciosa massif, and the refreshing Mingo Fountain. The trail also passes through beautiful Scots pine forests and by reservoirs.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during most seasons, but spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, so an early start is recommended. Winter hiking is possible but may require appropriate gear for snow and ice conditions.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on trails within Guadarrama National Park. However, they must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Please remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for Guadarrama National Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or permits required for general hiking on trails like this one within Guadarrama National Park. However, always check the official park regulations for any updates or specific restrictions, especially during peak seasons or for certain activities.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or water sources on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find the Mingo Fountain, considered one of the best water sources in the Sierra de Guadarrama, along the way. The starting point near the parking lot may also have basic facilities, but it's always wise to carry enough water and snacks for your hike.

faqs.question: Is this a family-friendly hike?
faqs.answer: While generally considered moderate, the varied terrain with some stony and steep sections might be challenging for very young children or those unaccustomed to hiking. Families with older children or those with good hiking experience should find it enjoyable, but assess your group's fitness level beforehand.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll be hiking along parts of the Senda del Valle de la Barranca and the Camino Ortiz, among others, offering a comprehensive experience of the area.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for steep sections, wear sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support. Layers of clothing are recommended as mountain weather can change quickly. Don't forget water, snacks, sun protection, and a map or GPS device like komoot for navigation.
